rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER WIENS ET AL VS. CHRISTOPHER COOKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Mental Examinations Of Plaintiffs Jennifer Wiens And Brian Mathy, Along With Attendant Request To Have This Motion Heard After The Deadline To Hear Discovery Motions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS CHRISTOPHER COOKE AND ANASTASSIOU HALEY'S MOTION TO COMPEL MENTAL EXAMINATIONS OF PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER WIENS AND BRIAN MATHY, ALONG WITH ATTENDANT REQUEST TO HAVE THIS MOTION HEARD AFTER THE DEADLINE TO HEAR DISCOVER MOTIONS is OFF CALENDAR. Action settled. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SALLY L. LIU VS. BRUCE R. BEASLEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANTS BRIAN GETZ, LORI GETZ, DANIEL ROMAN AND LC EQUITY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege any viable cause of action against the defendants. For each cause of action Plaintiff must allege facts (not conclusions) supporting each element of such cause of action. No cause of action may arise out of defendants' actions prior to loan origination (e.g. misrepresenting as to the proposed terms of the loan) or based on the matters appearing on the face of the Deed of Trust or Note (e.g. no causes of action arising out of unlicensed lending by individuals listed in Attachment A to the Deed of Trust recorded on July 15, 2016), as such claims are barred by the statute of limitations. Plaintiff may allege in good faith causes of action arising out of breach of the terms of the Note or Deed of Trust, if such breaches occurred within 4 years of filing of the complaint; or causes of action based on violations of relevant statutes (e.g. foreclosure statutes regarding). If Plaintiff choses to make such allegations, a copy of the Note shall be attached to the amended complaint. If Plaintiff proceeds with her claim for quiet title (as long as it is based on the grounds that are not time-barred), Plaintiff must allege tender the original amount of the loan (as stated in the Deed of Trust) that is not in dispute as having been received by Plaintiff. The Court does not express any opinion at this time as to propriety or viability of any causes of action that Plaintiff may be alleging. Any references to specific allegation that may be made (in good faith only) are made only as an example. All causes of action shall be stated in clear and concise language. While not required, Plaintiff is encouraged to include relevant factual allegations within each cause of action. Such formatting of the operative complaint will ensure that all elements of each cause of action are properly alleged. Plaintiff shall have 60 days from notice of entry of order to file her amended complaint. Defendant to prepare an order in accordance with CRC 3.1312(a) and (b). If defendant fails to prepare an order as stated above, Plaintiff to prepare an order in accordance with CRC 3.1312(d). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SALLY L. LIU VS. BRUCE R. BEASLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Re Plaintiff Sally L. Liu And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 23, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANTS BRIAN GETZ, LORI GETZ, DANIEL ROMAN AND LC EQUITY'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS RE PLAINTIFF SALLY LIU AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS is DENIED. Defendant to prepare and submit a proposed Order Granting Motion to Compel heard on May 25, 2021. Proposed order must be submitted for review as to form to Plaintiff no later than August 23, 2021. Defendant to submit the proposed order to execution and entry to the Court with proof of compliance with CRC 3.1312 no later than September 3, 2021. Defendant to Notice a deposition in this action for September 23, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
